title: Smoky Roasted Catalan Chicken Paella
description: A hearty, smoky, and crunchy Spanish-inspired dish featuring molasses and al-dente rice, perfect for a lazy Sunday lunch. This recipe is egg-free and features a variety of smoky flavors from the chicken and vegetable marinade.

ingredients.name: chicken broth
ingredients.notes: low-sodium, for cooking rice and chicken

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the vegetable oil, smoked paprika, ground cumin, and salt. Add the chicken pieces and toss to coat evenly.
Heat a large paella pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
Add the vegetable oil to the pan and swirl to coat. Add the Spanish rice and cook, stirring constantly, until the rice is lightly toasted, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chicken broth to the pan and bring to a boil. Add the browned chicken back to the pan and stir to combine.
Transfer the pan to the preheated oven and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
While the paella is cooking, prepare the molasses mixture by whisking together the molasses and 1 tablespoon of water in a small bowl.
Remove the pan from the oven and stir in the molasses mixture. Sprinkle with black pepper to taste.
Let the paella rest for 5-10 minutes before serving. Cover with foil to keep warm.
Serve hot, garnished with lemon wedges, if desired.
